annotations.label: Dynamical system (informal)

anchor.snippet: a dynamical system is the description of how a system evolves in time

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Flow` formalizes a dynamical system as a continuous monoid action, capturing the informal idea of time evolution.

annotations.label: Trajectory / orbit

anchor.snippet: a collection of points known as a trajectory or orbit

annotations.note: `Flow.orbit` is defined as the set `{ϕ t x | t : τ}` for a point `x` under a flow `ϕ`.

annotations.label: Simple examples of dynamical systems

anchor.snippet: Simple examples include the mathematical models that describe the swinging of a clock pendulum

annotations.note: No pendulum, water-flow, or analogous physical-model example is formalized in `Mathlib/Dynamics`.

annotations.label: Three body problem

anchor.snippet: Three body problem

annotations.note: The three-body problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arnold cat map

anchor.snippet: Arnold cat map

annotations.note: Arnold's cat map is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Baker's map

anchor.snippet: Baker's map

annotations.note: The baker's map is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bunimovich stadium billiards

anchor.snippet: A particle moving inside the Bunimovich stadium

annotations.note: Bunimovich stadium billiards are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Outer billiards

anchor.snippet: Outer billiards

annotations.note: Outer billiards are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bouncing ball dynamics

anchor.snippet: Bouncing ball dynamics

annotations.note: Bouncing-ball dynamics are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circle map bifurcation diagram

anchor.snippet: Bifurcation diagram for a Circle map

annotations.note: Circle-map bifurcation diagrams are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex quadratic polynomial dynamical system

anchor.snippet: The recursive application of a Complex quadratic polynomial

annotations.note: Iteration of complex quadratic polynomials (Julia/Mandelbrot setting) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Double compound pendulum

anchor.snippet: Motion of the double compound pendulum

annotations.note: The double compound pendulum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dyadic transformation

anchor.snippet: Dyadic transformation xy plot

annotations.note: The dyadic transformation (doubling map) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorenz attractor

anchor.snippet: The Lorenz attractor arises in the study of the Lorenz oscillator

annotations.note: The Lorenz attractor/oscillator is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kármán vortex street

anchor.snippet: An example of a Kármán vortex street

annotations.note: The Kármán vortex street is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kicked Rotor

anchor.snippet: The Kicked Rotor , a famous chaotic system

annotations.note: The kicked rotor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orbital resonance in Saturn's rings

anchor.snippet: Orbital resonance in Saturn's rings

annotations.note: Orbital resonance is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chaotic rotation of Hyperion

anchor.snippet: The chaotic rotation of Hyperion

annotations.note: The chaotic rotation of Hyperion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré recurrence theorem

anchor.snippet: These papers included the Poincaré recurrence theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Mathlib.Dynamics.Ergodic.Conservative` contains both the measure-theoretic and topological versions of Poincaré recurrence.

annotations.label: Birkhoff ergodic theorem

anchor.snippet: his 1931 discovery of what is now called the ergodic theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has the von Neumann mean ergodic theorem and `birkhoffSum`/`birkhoffAverage` infrastructure but not Birkhoff's pointwise ergodic theorem.

annotations.label: Sharkovsky's theorem

anchor.snippet: developed Sharkovsky's theorem on the periods of discrete dynamical systems

annotations.note: Sharkovsky's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: State space as abstract set

anchor.snippet: the state space X is treated as a generic set of abstract algebra

annotations.note: In `Flow`, the state space `α` is taken as an abstract (topological) type, matching the informal description.

annotations.label: Generalized state space

anchor.snippet: It is possible to allow different choices of the state space such as a function space

annotations.note: `Flow` is parametric in the topological state space, so any function/manifold/measurable space can be used, but no special instances are catalogued.

annotations.label: Multidimensional systems

anchor.snippet: A general class of systems are defined over multiple independent variables and are therefore called multidimensional systems

annotations.note: Dynamical systems with multidimensional time are not specifically formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete dynamical system (time)

anchor.snippet: Time can also be a discrete parameter

annotations.note: `Flow.fromIter` makes a `Flow ℕ α` from any continuous self-map, formalizing discrete-time dynamics.

annotations.label: Deterministic evolution rule

anchor.snippet: The evolution rule of the dynamical system is a function that describes what future states follow from the current state

annotations.note: The deterministic evolution rule is realized as `Flow.toFun : τ → α → α`.

annotations.label: Stochastic dynamical system

anchor.snippet: Some systems are also stochastic

annotations.note: Mathlib has Markov kernels but no general stochastic-dynamical-system type.

annotations.label: Chaotic and quantum systems

anchor.snippet: Last but not least there are chaotic systems

annotations.note: No definition of chaotic/quantum dynamical system as such appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formal dynamical system (general transformation)

anchor.snippet: Assume that X is a non empty set with elements called states

annotations.note: `Flow τ α` packages the state space, time monoid, and transformation, matching the formal definition.

anchors.snippet: Assume that X is a non empty set with elements called states

annotations.label: Geometrical definition of dynamical system

anchor.snippet: In the geometrical definition, a dynamical system is the tuple

annotations.note: `Flow` captures the (T, M, Φ) tuple at the topological level; the smooth-manifold variant is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Algebraic dynamical system

anchor.snippet: An important class of systems from a mathematical perspective is when the map

annotations.note: An algebraic-dynamical-system class is not specifically defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real dynamical system / flow

anchor.snippet: A real dynamical system , real-time dynamical system , continuous time dynamical system , or flow is a tuple

annotations.note: Instantiating `Flow ℝ α` gives the real-time flow tuple `(ℝ, M, Φ)` of the definition.

annotations.label: Differentiable dynamical system

anchor.snippet: If Φ is continuously differentiable the system is called a differentiable dynamical system

annotations.note: No `DifferentiableFlow`/`SmoothFlow` predicate combining `Flow` with differentiability is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite- vs infinite-dimensional

anchor.snippet: the dynamical system is finite-dimensional ; if not, the dynamical system is infinite-dimensional

annotations.note: No specific finite-/infinite-dimensional classification of dynamical systems is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Global flow and semi-flow

anchor.snippet: When T is taken to be the reals, the dynamical system is called global or a flow

annotations.note: `Flow τ α` covers both the global (τ = ℝ) and the semi-flow (τ = ℕ or ℝ≥0) cases without separate naming.

annotations.label: Discrete-time dynamical system

anchor.snippet: A discrete-time dynamical system is a tuple

annotations.note: `Flow.fromIter` constructs a `Flow ℕ α` from a continuous self-map, realizing the discrete-time tuple.

annotations.label: Measure-theoretic dynamical system

anchor.snippet: A dynamical system may be defined formally as a measure-preserving transformation of a measure space

annotations.note: `MeasurePreserving f μ μ` formalizes a measure-preserving transformation, the standard measure-theoretic dynamical system.

annotations.label: Krylov–Bogolyubov theorem

anchor.snippet: A simple construction (sometimes called the Krylov–Bogolyubov theorem )

annotations.note: The Krylov–Bogolyubov existence-of-invariant-measure theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sinai–Ruelle–Bowen measures

anchor.snippet: For hyperbolic dynamical systems, the Sinai–Ruelle–Bowen measures appear to be the natural choice

annotations.note: SRB measures are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological dynamical system

anchor.snippet: A topological dynamical system is a global dynamical system

annotations.note: `Flow` is exactly a topological flow; there is no separately named `TopologicalDynamicalSystem` structure.

annotations.label: Limit set in compact dynamical systems

anchor.snippet: In compact dynamical systems the limit set of any orbit is non-empty

annotations.note: `nonempty_omegaLimit` shows the ω-limit set is nonempty over a compact space and nonempty starting set.

annotations.label: Category as dynamical system

anchor.snippet: A category X of mathematical objects has a semigroup G of homomorphisms acting on it

annotations.note: Mathlib has categories and semigroup/monoid actions but no formal `Category as dynamical system` definition.

annotations.label: Dynamical system via monoid action

anchor.snippet: A dynamical system

annotations.note: `Flow` extends naturally to an `AddAction τ α` via `Flow.toAddAction`, matching the monoid-action definition.

annotations.label: Evolution function

anchor.snippet: The function Φ( t , x ) is called the evolution function of the dynamical system

annotations.note: `Flow.toFun : τ → α → α` is exactly the evolution function Φ(t,x).

annotations.label: Flow, trajectory, orbit through x

anchor.snippet: is called the flow through x and its graph is called the trajectory through x

annotations.note: `Flow.orbit ϕ x` is `{ϕ t x : t ∈ τ}`, the set-theoretic flow/orbit through `x`.

anchors.snippet: is called the flow through x and its graph is called the trajectory through x

annotations.label: Φ-invariant subset

anchor.snippet: A subset S of the state space X is called Φ- invariant

annotations.note: `IsInvariant ϕ s` is defined as `∀ t, MapsTo (ϕ t) s s`, the standard Φ-invariance condition.

anchors.snippet: A subset S of the state space X is called Φ- invariant

annotations.label: Construction from ODE initial value problem

anchor.snippet: consider an initial value problem such as the following

annotations.note: Mathlib has ODE existence/uniqueness (Picard–Lindelöf) but no construction packaging the solution as a `Flow`.

anchors.snippet: consider an initial value problem such as the following

annotations.label: Autonomous and homogeneous systems

anchor.snippet: autonomous , when v ( t , x ) = v ( x )

annotations.note: No predicates labelling ODEs as autonomous or homogeneous are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete dynamical system (overall)

anchor.snippet: A discrete dynamical system is when either time or space or both are discrete

annotations.note: `Flow.fromIter` formalizes a discrete-time dynamical system via iteration of a continuous map.

annotations.label: Discrete map as dynamical system

anchor.snippet: it is possible to define the dynamical system as a general discrete map

annotations.note: Iteration `f^[n]` together with `Flow.fromIter` packages a discrete map as a dynamical system.

annotations.label: Fibonacci Rabbits

anchor.snippet: Fibonacci Rabbits

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Fibonacci sequence but not its presentation as a dynamical system / rabbit-population model.

annotations.label: Logistic map

anchor.snippet: Logistic map A generic map applied to itself recursively

annotations.note: The logistic map `x ↦ r x (1-x)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete predator-prey model

anchor.snippet: Discrete example of predator prey model

annotations.note: Discrete predator–prey dynamics are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PageRank

anchor.snippet: PageRank is an example of discrete dynamical system

annotations.note: PageRank is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite difference equation system

anchor.snippet: the starting point is typically a finite difference equation

annotations.note: A general finite-difference-equation framework for dynamical systems is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the starting point is typically a finite difference equation

annotations.label: Cascade and semi-cascade

anchor.snippet: Discrete dynamical systems are often also called cascades

annotations.note: Mathlib does not use the terminology cascade / semi-cascade.

annotations.label: Cellular automaton

anchor.snippet: A cellular automaton is a tuple

annotations.note: Cellular automata are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear dynamical system

anchor.snippet: In a linear system the phase space is the N -dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: There is no dedicated `LinearDynamicalSystem` predicate combining `Flow` with linearity in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Superposition principle for linear systems

anchor.snippet: they satisfy a superposition principle

annotations.note: Linearity itself (additivity and scaling) is general infrastructure (`LinearMap`), but no statement frames it as the dynamical-system superposition principle.

annotations.label: Affine linear flow

anchor.snippet: For a flow , the vector field v( x ) is an affine function of the position in the phase space

annotations.note: Affine vector-field flows are not specifically defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: For a flow , the vector field v( x ) is an affine function of the position in the phase space

annotations.label: Equilibrium and exponential solution

anchor.snippet: When b is zero and A ≠ 0 the origin is an equilibrium (or singular) point of the flow

annotations.note: Equilibrium points and matrix-exponential solutions of linear flows are not packaged as dynamical-system statements in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: When b is zero and A ≠ 0 the origin is an equilibrium (or singular) point of the flow

annotations.label: Sensitive dependence in linear divergence

anchor.snippet: Linear systems display sensitive dependence on initial conditions in the case of divergence

annotations.note: Sensitive dependence on initial conditions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine discrete-time map

anchor.snippet: A discrete-time , affine dynamical system has the form of a matrix difference equation

annotations.note: Affine matrix-difference-equation dynamical systems are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A discrete-time , affine dynamical system has the form of a matrix difference equation

annotations.label: Invariant curve from eigenvector

anchor.snippet: if u 1 is an eigenvector of A , with a real eigenvalue smaller than one, then the straight lines

annotations.note: Invariant lines from eigenvectors under linear maps are not stated as a dynamical-system result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Qualitative invariance under smooth changes

anchor.snippet: The qualitative properties of dynamical systems do not change under a smooth change of coordinates

annotations.note: Conjugacy/qualitative-invariance under smooth coordinate changes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rectification theorem

anchor.snippet: This is known as the rectification theorem

annotations.note: The flow-box / rectification theorem for vector fields is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré section and Poincaré map

anchor.snippet: These points are a Poincaré section

annotations.note: Poincaré sections and Poincaré return maps are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugation equation and non-resonant condition

anchor.snippet: This is known as the conjugation equation

annotations.note: The conjugation equation and non-resonance conditions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic and elliptic fixed points

anchor.snippet: When the eigenvalues of J are not in the unit circle, the dynamics near the fixed point

annotations.note: Hyperbolic / elliptic fixed-point classifications are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hartman–Grobman theorem

anchor.snippet: the Hartman–Grobman theorem gives the conditions for the existence of a continuous function

annotations.note: The Hartman–Grobman theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov–Arnold–Moser theorem

anchor.snippet: The Kolmogorov–Arnold–Moser (KAM) theorem gives the behavior near an elliptic point

annotations.note: The KAM theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bifurcation

anchor.snippet: At this point the phase space changes qualitatively and the dynamical system is said to have gone through a bifurcation

annotations.note: Bifurcation theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bifurcation characterization by eigenvalues

anchor.snippet: The bifurcations of a hyperbolic fixed point

annotations.note: Eigenvalue characterization of bifurcations is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ruelle–Takens and Feigenbaum scenarios

anchor.snippet: the Ruelle–Takens scenario describes how a periodic orbit bifurcates into a torus

annotations.note: The Ruelle–Takens and Feigenbaum scenarios are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville measure / phase-space invariance

anchor.snippet: The volume is said to be computed by the Liouville measure

annotations.note: The Liouville measure on phase space and Hamiltonian phase-space invariance are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The volume is said to be computed by the Liouville measure

annotations.label: Poincaré recurrence theorem (ergodic statement)

anchor.snippet: Poincaré discovered the recurrence theorem

annotations.note: The measure-theoretic Poincaré recurrence theorem is proved in `Mathlib.Dynamics.Ergodic.Conservative`.

annotations.label: Ergodic hypothesis

anchor.snippet: the possible equality between time averages and space averages, what he called the ergodic hypothesis

annotations.note: `Ergodic` captures the modern ergodicity property, but the time/space-average equivalence statement (Birkhoff pointwise theorem) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transfer (Koopman) operator

anchor.snippet: This introduces an operator U t , the transfer operator

annotations.note: The Koopman/transfer operator is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: This introduces an operator U t , the transfer operator

annotations.label: SRB measures

anchor.snippet: SRB measures replace the Boltzmann factor

annotations.note: Sinai–Ruelle–Bowen measures are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic / chaotic systems

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic systems are precisely defined dynamical systems that exhibit the properties ascribed to chaotic systems

annotations.note: Hyperbolic / chaotic dynamical systems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solutions of finite duration

anchor.snippet: it is possible under some conditions to develop solutions of finite duration

annotations.note: Finite-duration solutions to ODEs are not formalized as a dynamical-systems concept in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite duration solution example

anchor.snippet: Admits the finite duration solution

annotations.note: Specific finite-duration ODE example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic dynamical system (general)

anchor.snippet: Algebraic dynamical systems can be considered as a special algebraic case of the classical geometric definition

annotations.note: An algebraic-dynamical-system class is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poncelet map

anchor.snippet: One such examples is the Poncelet map

annotations.note: The Poncelet map is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic billiard

anchor.snippet: Another example can be a billiard with a border that is an algebraic curve

annotations.note: Elliptic (algebraic-curve) billiards are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ergodic decomposition analogy

anchor.snippet: It is also possible to draw an analogy between group representation theory

annotations.note: The ergodic-decomposition analogy with representation theory is not formalized in Mathlib.
